rag_rules: Place input assumptions in component type annexes when they describe interface behavior
Use implementation annexes only for implementation-level bindings like assignments
Assume/guarantee/eq/const clauses belong in component type annexes
Assign/assert clauses belong in component implementation annexes
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for assumptions and guarantees
Place assumptions and guarantees in the scope where their referenced features are visible
Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, equations, constants, and assignments
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks use `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equations
Use `const name : type = value;` for constants
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without proper exposure
Do not mix component type and implementation clauses in the same annex block
